([Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 申思; born 1 May 1973 in [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai")) is a former [Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_people "Chinese people") international [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who played as a left midfielder where he gained a reputation for his technical and free kick skills, which saw him become a runner-up in the 2004 [Free Kick Masters](/wiki/Free_Kick_Masters "Free Kick Masters"). After representing [Shanghai Shenhua](/wiki/Shanghai_Shenhua "Shanghai Shenhua") and [Shanghai International](/wiki/Shanghai_International "Shanghai International") as a player he took up managing [Pudong Zobon F.C.](/wiki/Pudong_Zobon_F.C. "Pudong Zobon F.C.") before being relieved of his position after it was revealed that he was involved in fixing a game in the 2003 [Chinese Jia-A League](/wiki/Chinese_Jia-A_League "Chinese Jia-A League") season while he was a player. On October 15, 2010, he was detained by the police for [his involvement in fixing a league game](/wiki/2003%E2%80%932009_Chinese_football_match-fixing_scandals#Shanghai_International_vs_Tianjin_TEDA_in_2003 "2003–2009 Chinese football match-fixing scandals") against [Tianjin Teda](/wiki/Tianjin_Jinmen_Tiger_F.C. "Tianjin Jinmen Tiger F.C.") on November 30, 2003 and was sentenced to six years imprisonment on June 13, 2012.

**Shen Si** ([Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"): 申思; born 1 May 1973 in [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai")) is a former [Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_people "Chinese people") international [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who played as a left midfielder where he gained a reputation for his technical and free kick skills, which saw him become a runner-up in the 2004 [Free Kick Masters](/wiki/Free_Kick_Masters "Free Kick Masters"). After representing [Shanghai Shenhua](/wiki/Shanghai_Shenhua "Shanghai Shenhua") and [Shanghai International](/wiki/Shanghai_International "Shanghai International") as a player he took up managing [Pudong Zobon F.C.](/wiki/Pudong_Zobon_F.C. "Pudong Zobon F.C.") before being relieved of his position after it was revealed that he was involved in fixing a game in the 2003 [Chinese Jia-A League](/wiki/Chinese_Jia-A_League "Chinese Jia-A League") season while he was a player. On October 15, 2010, he was detained by the police for [his involvement in fixing a league game](/wiki/2003%E2%80%932009_Chinese_football_match-fixing_scandals#Shanghai_International_vs_Tianjin_TEDA_in_2003 "2003–2009 Chinese football match-fixing scandals") against [Tianjin Teda](/wiki/Tianjin_Jinmen_Tiger_F.C. "Tianjin Jinmen Tiger F.C.") on November 30, 2003 and was sentenced to six years imprisonment on June 13, 2012.

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shen_Si&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Playing career")]

Shen Si would go to several specialist sports schools during his youth before being picked up by [Shanghai Shenhua](/wiki/Shanghai_Shenhua "Shanghai Shenhua") and the [Chinese U-23 team](/wiki/China_national_under-23_football_team "China national under-23 football team"). At Shenhua he would start his career at the dawn of China's first fully professional league season in the [1994 league season](/wiki/Chinese_Jia-A_League_1994 "Chinese Jia-A League 1994") where he quickly establish himself as a vital player within the squad before going on to win the league title in the [1995 league season](/wiki/Chinese_Jia-A_League_1995 "Chinese Jia-A League 1995").[[2]](#cite_note-2) At Shenhua, Shen would add the 1998 [Chinese FA Cup](/wiki/Chinese_FA_Cup "Chinese FA Cup") medal to his collection and become a regular for the [Chinese national team](/wiki/China_PR_national_football_team "China PR national football team"), which saw him included in the [2000 AFC Asian Cup](/wiki/2000_AFC_Asian_Cup "2000 AFC Asian Cup") in a tournament that saw China finish fourth. Despite becoming the club's captain at Shenhua by 2001 in 2002 Shen decided to make a big money move to local rivals [Shanghai International](/wiki/Shanghai_International "Shanghai International") who were willing to pay 9.4 million [Yuan](/wiki/Chinese_yuan "Chinese yuan") and wages equivalent to 20,000 [US dollars](/wiki/United_States_dollar "United States dollar") per week to make Shen one of the best paid players in Chinese football. His move would eventually see Shanghai International become title contenders and they just missed out of the title at the end of the [2003 league season](/wiki/Chinese_Jia-A_League_2003 "Chinese Jia-A League 2003").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The following season saw Shen spend most of the season injured, due to his age and his wages the club would off load him to second tier club [Shanghai Zobon](/wiki/Shanghai_Zobon "Shanghai Zobon") where Shen would eventually retire.

## International goals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shen_Si&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International goals")]

| No. | Date | Venue | Opponent | Score | Result | Competition |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1. | 26 January 2000 | [Thống Nhất Stadium](/wiki/Th%E1%BB%91ng_Nh%E1%BA%A5t_Stadium "Thống Nhất Stadium"), [Ho Chi Minh City](/wiki/Ho_Chi_Minh_City "Ho Chi Minh City"), [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam") | [Guam](/wiki/Guam_national_football_team "Guam national football team") | **17**–0 | 19–0 | [2000 AFC Asian Cup qualification](/wiki/2000_AFC_Asian_Cup_qualification "2000 AFC Asian Cup qualification") |

## Management career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shen_Si&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Management career")]

He became the care-taker manager of [Pudong Zobon](/wiki/Pudong_Zobon_F.C. "Pudong Zobon F.C.") during the 2009 season and later was offered the position on a permanent basis. Under his stewardship, the team ranked 9th in the [China League One](/wiki/China_League_One "China League One") at the end of season. In the 2010 season Shen Si led his team to a flying start but experienced a string of mediocre results afterward. Throughout the term [Pudong Zobon](/wiki/Pudong_Zobon_F.C. "Pudong Zobon F.C.") was fighting against relegation. On October 15, 2010, he was reported to have been detained by the police and was said to be involved in fixing the game during his stint at [Shanghai Zhongyuan](/wiki/Shanghai_Zhongyuan "Shanghai Zhongyuan"). He was absent from the subsequent matches of [Pudong Zobon](/wiki/Pudong_Zobon_F.C. "Pudong Zobon F.C.").[[4]](#cite_note-4)
Shortly afterward the club announced that his place was taken by the assistant manager [Huang Zhenhua](/wiki/Huang_Zhenhua "Huang Zhenhua"). At the end of the 2010 season, [Pudong Zobon](/wiki/Pudong_Zobon_F.C. "Pudong Zobon F.C.") won its battle against relegation and ranked 10th.

## Match-fixing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shen_Si&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Match-fixing")]

See also: [2003–2009 Chinese football match-fixing scandals § Shanghai International vs Tianjin TEDA in 2003](/wiki/2003%E2%80%932009_Chinese_football_match-fixing_scandals#Shanghai_International_vs_Tianjin_TEDA_in_2003 "2003–2009 Chinese football match-fixing scandals")

On October 15, 2010, Shen was reported to have been detained by the police and was said to be involved in fixing the November 30, 2003 league game against [Tianjin Teda](/wiki/Tianjin_Jinmen_Tiger_F.C. "Tianjin Jinmen Tiger F.C.") during his stint as a player at Shanghai International. The allegations suggests that Shen was bribed by former Tianjin Teda general manager Yang Yifeng a total of 12 million Yuan to lose the game and that Shen had asked teammates [Qi Hong](/wiki/Qi_Hong "Qi Hong"), [Jiang Jin](/wiki/Jiang_Jin "Jiang Jin") and [Li Ming (1975)](/wiki/Li_Ming_(footballer,_born_1975) "Li Ming (footballer, born 1975)") to help him.[[5]](#cite_note-5) After being arrested by the police Qi Hong along with Shen were later released on bail while the police traced back 2 million Yuan from their accounts, after a lengthy wait Shen was found guilty of match-fixing as was sentenced to six years imprisonment on June 13, 2012 and fined 500,000 Yuan while his associates were given five and a half each.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Shen_Si&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Honours")]
